course_description:
Cells are the basic units of life. In this course, you will learn how molecular mechanisms control the essential processes that allow cells to function. The course explores the pathways and proteins that regulate cell proliferation, communication, movement, and death. Understanding how these processes are disrupted in disease is central to the development of genetic and drug-based therapies for cancer, diabetes, and metabolic disorders.

This course is intended to be a comprehensive introduction to cell biology. Some of the main concepts covered include:

The course has 3 one-hour lectures per week.ﾠ All lectures are recorded and made available on Blackboard for delayed viewing.

Students will attend one 3-hour practical class per week for a total of six weeks. The practicals will focus on microscopical protein detection and functional analysis of gene knockout, practical laboratory skills, planning and executing experiments, accurate recording of data, interpretation of data, free thinking and decision-making by the students.ﾠ

learning_outcomes.description: Describe and explain the major components of cells, including their molecular structures and functions, and apply the principle of compartmentalisation to relate spatial organisation to cellular processes.
Explain and interpret how cells detect and respond to external signals, and analyse cellular responses using defined signalling pathways as exemplars.
Employ cell biology techniques to investigate changes in cell morphology associated with disease phenotypes.
Collaborate effectively in a team to design, conduct and evaluate cell biology experiments, applying appropriate scientific reasoning and communication skills.

assessment_details.due_date: 2/04/2026 The online quiz will be available for a 24 hour period. The quiz will be open at 9 am for 24 hours prior to the due date. More detailed information on how to access the quiz will be provided on Blackboard closer to the time of assessment.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: This quiz will assess content covered in practical 1. More detailed information will be provided on Blackboard and/or during practicals. Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. Online via Blackboard.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to defer this exam .
Extensions are NOT applicable to this assessment item. To request a deferred quiz, students are referred to the relevant UQ policy for reasons acceptable for a deferred quiz. A SAP is not sufficient to be granted a deferred quiz.
Late submission
Exams submitted after the end of the submission time will incur a late penalty .
For submissions:
less than 5 minutes late: 5% penalty between 5 minutes to less than 15 minutes late: 20% penalty more than 15 minutes late: 100% penalty.

assessment_details.title: In-Semester Exam
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: This exam will test your knowledge of module 1 (Organelles), module 2 (Membranes) and module 3 (Cytoskeleton) of the course. Further information on the structure of the exam will be provided closer to the assessment date. This will be a closed book, in-person, invigilated exam. This means you are not permitted any course material or any other reference material or resources. Detailed instructions will be provided on Blackboard closer to the examination. Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13
Multiple weeks From Week 2 To Week 5
learning_activities.topic: Practical 1 (SBMS) Practical 1 is coordinated by A/Prof Rohan Teasdale at the School of Biomedical Sciences. Practical 1 takes two weeks. Prac groups 1-6 will attend practical classes in teaching weeks 2 and 3. Prac groups 7-12 will attend practical classes in teaching weeks 4 and 5. Please carefully check which practical class you are enrolled in, and then check the practical timetable on Blackboard for the dates for your practical classes.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 6 To Week 9
learning_activities.topic: Practical 2 (SCMB) Practical 2 is coordinated by Dr Mathew Jones at the School of Chemistry and Molecular Biosciences. Practical 2 takes four weeks. Please carefully check which practical class you are enrolled in, and then check the practical timetable on Blackboard for the dates for your practical classes.

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments for Students Policy and Procedure AI for Assessment Guide Recording of Teaching Policy and Procedure Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library . You'll also need to be aware of the following policies and procedures while completing this course: Occupational Health and Safety in the Laboratory Guideline Learning activities
